old_claim.note: Motorized Logistics supports manufacturing, deployment, commercialization, or operational scaling.

new_claim.note: The scoped fleet capability operates on motor vehicles whose movements, routes, dispatch, and deliveries are tracked and managed with GPS.

source_shape.source_locator: Commercial Applications and Productivity Enhancement sections: describes GPS vehicle navigation, vehicle-fleet tracking and management, commercial routing and dispatch, and delivery-time and fuel savings.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The federal report documents the scoped GPS application through motor-vehicle fleets, their routes, dispatch decisions, and deliveries.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: For a node explicitly limited to vehicle-fleet logistics, removing motorized transport removes the operational objects being positioned and managed, not merely a later channel for scaling.

ontology_before: Motorized logistics was treated only as commercialization support for GPS fleet operations.
ontology_after: Motor vehicles and their logistics operations are required constituents of the scoped GPS-guided fleet capability.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: motorized_logistics remains required for gps_guided_logistics.
would_reject_if: The node is broadened to include GPS tracking of non-vehicle assets, people, or wildlife as equivalent instances.
The scope is narrowed to GPS map software independent of motorized fleet operation.
short_reason: The scoped capability is specifically GPS management of motor-vehicle fleets.
